title: An off switch for dual-use knowledge in AI models
excerpt: Anthropic alignment research introducing a mechanism to selectively disable dual-use knowledge in AI models.
filter_reason: Anthropic alignment research on selectively disabling dual-use knowledge - a substantive safety-capability mechanism from a major lab.
summary: Anthropic published alignment research introducing an 'off switch' for dual-use knowledge: a mechanism that can selectively disable hazardous knowledge (e.g., bio or cyber uplift) inside a model without retraining it from scratch. This matters because unlearning and knowledge gating have been notoriously brittle, and a working selective-disable mechanism would change how labs handle dangerous-capability thresholds. It is a concrete safety-engineering advance from a frontier lab, not a policy statement.
